comment_text.ud6.5:10.2: Attā ca loko ca here and following is missing from the parallel passage in DN 29:36.9. I assume it has been inserted in error and translate accordingly.

root_text.ud6.5:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud6.5:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā nānātitthiyasamaṇabrāhmaṇaparibbājakā sāvatthiyaṁ paṭivasanti nānādiṭṭhikā nānākhantikā nānārucikā nānādiṭṭhinissayanissitā.
root_text.ud6.5:2.2: “sassato attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:3.2: “asassato attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:4.2: “sassato ca asassato ca attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:5.2: “neva sassato nāsassato attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:6.2: “sayaṅkato attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:7.2: “paraṅkato attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:8.2: “sayaṅkato ca paraṅkato ca attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:9.2: “asayaṅkāro aparaṅkāro adhiccasamuppanno attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:10.2: “sassataṁ sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:11.2: “asassataṁ sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:12.2: “sassatañca asassatañca sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:13.2: “neva sassataṁ nāsassataṁ sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:14.2: “sayaṅkataṁ sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:15.2: “paraṅkataṁ sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:16.2: “sayaṅkatañca paraṅkatañca sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:17.2: “asayaṅkāraṁ aparaṅkāraṁ adhiccasamuppannaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.5:18.1: Te bhaṇḍanajātā kalahajātā vivādāpannā aññamaññaṁ mukhasattīhi vitudantā viharanti:
root_text.ud6.5:19.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁsu.
root_text.ud6.5:19.2: Sāvatthiyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.ud6.5:19.3: Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.ud6.5:20.1: “Idha, bhante, sambahulā nānātitthiyasamaṇabrāhmaṇaparibbājakā sāvatthiyaṁ paṭivasanti nānādiṭṭhikā nānākhantikā nānārucikā nānādiṭṭhinissayanissitā.
root_text.ud6.5:21.2: ‘sassato attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti …pe…
root_text.ud6.5:21.3: te bhaṇḍanajātā kalahajātā vivādāpannā aññamaññaṁ mukhasattīhi vitudantā viharanti:
root_text.ud6.5:22.2: atthaṁ na jānanti anatthaṁ na jānanti, dhammaṁ na jānanti adhammaṁ na jānanti.
root_text.ud6.5:22.3: Te atthaṁ ajānantā anatthaṁ ajānantā, dhammaṁ ajānantā adhammaṁ ajānantā bhaṇḍanajātā kalahajātā vivādāpannā aññamaññaṁ mukhasattīhi vitudantā viharanti:
root_text.ud6.5:23.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud6.5:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud6.5:1.3: Now at that time several ascetics, brahmins, and wanderers who followed various other religions were residing in Sāvatthī, holding different views, beliefs, and opinions, relying on different views.
translation_text.ud6.5:2.1: There were some ascetics and brahmins who had this doctrine and view:
translation_text.ud6.5:2.2: “The self and the cosmos are eternal. This is the only truth, anything else is futile.”
translation_text.ud6.5:3.1: Others held views such as the following, each regarding their own view as true and others as silly.
translation_text.ud6.5:3.2: “The self and the cosmos are not eternal.”
translation_text.ud6.5:4.2: “The self and the cosmos are both eternal and not eternal.”
translation_text.ud6.5:5.2: “The self and the cosmos are neither eternal nor not eternal.”
translation_text.ud6.5:6.2: “The self and the cosmos are made by oneself.”
translation_text.ud6.5:7.2: “The self and the cosmos are made by another.”
translation_text.ud6.5:8.2: “The self and the cosmos are made by both oneself and another.”
translation_text.ud6.5:9.2: “The self and the cosmos have arisen anomalously, not made by oneself or another.”
translation_text.ud6.5:10.2: “Pleasure and pain are eternal, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.5:11.2: “Pleasure and pain are not eternal, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.5:12.2: “Pleasure and pain are both eternal and not eternal, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.5:13.2: “Pleasure and pain are neither eternal nor not eternal, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.5:14.2: “Pleasure and pain are made by oneself, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.5:15.2: “Pleasure and pain are made by another, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.5:16.2: “Pleasure and pain are made by both oneself and another, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.5:17.2: “Pleasure and pain have arisen anomalously, not made by oneself or another, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.5:18.1: They were fighting, quarreling, and disputing, continually wounding each other with barbed words:
translation_text.ud6.5:18.2: “Such is Truth, such is not Truth!
translation_text.ud6.5:18.3: Such is not Truth, such is Truth!”
translation_text.ud6.5:19.1: Then several mendicants robed up in the morning and, taking their bowls and robes, entered Sāvatthī for alms.
translation_text.ud6.5:19.2: Then, after the meal, when they returned from almsround, they went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what was happening.
translation_text.ud6.5:23.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:

variant_text.ud6.5:4.2: sassato ca asassato ca → sassato asassato (bj, pts-vp-pli1)
variant_text.ud6.5:8.2: sayaṅkato ca paraṅkato ca → sayaṅkato paraṅkato (bj)
variant_text.ud6.5:9.2: asayaṅkāro aparaṅkāro → asayaṅkāro ca aparaṅkāro ca (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1)
